summary: Dormant obfuscated constants and BMP LSB steganography reader in schemavault
details: schemavault/_registry.py ships three integer-array constants (_ep, _p, _k) shaped like an encoded string plus a 16-byte key, embedded among plausible-looking transport-config values. schemavault/imaging.py exposes extract_metadata(), a BMP LSB steganography reader that pulls a length-prefixed string out of the least significant bits of the shipped assets/logo.bmp. Neither capability is reached from __init__.py, setup.py, or any other auto-executed code path in this package, so installing or importing schemavault 4.1.1 by itself does not exfiltrate data, execute remote code, or drop persistence. However, the combined shape — hidden constants, an embedded-key-shaped byte array, a stego reader over a shipped bitmap, and a declared dependency on an external package ('bytekit') that could invoke the decoder — matches a staged-payload pattern where the activation lives in a companion package rather than in this one. Warrants human review to determine whether the 'bytekit' dependency (or any other consumer) invokes extract_metadata/_registry constants to reconstitute an executable payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
